items.title: Ange: Frank sacking 'fair,' but Spurs lack vision
items.summary: Ange Postecoglou has labelled Thomas Frank's sacking a "fair departure" but sympathised with the Dane for having to work in an "environment of uncertainty."

items.title: Wrexham's Parkinson welcomes expanded playoffs
items.summary: Wrexham boss Phil Parkinson has welcomed the prospect of an expanded play-off system in the Championship, insisting there is no shortage of Premier League potential in the division.

items.title: FA: Tuchel's new deal not related to United job
items.summary: Football Association chief executive Mark Bullingham said there are "performance conditions" connected to Thomas Tuchel staying on as England head coach beyond the World Cup.

items.title: Dyche out as Forest sack 3rd coach this season
items.summary: Nottingham Forest sacked coach Sean Dyche on Wednesday, following his team's 0-0 draw with Premier League bottom-club Wolves, the team announced.

items.title: 'Bayern en Chelsea strijden om Verbruggen, De Zerbi kandidaat bij Spurs'
items.summary: We zitten in een periode tussen twee transferwindows, maar de stroom aan transfernieuws houdt nooit op. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Voetbaltop eensgezind op UEFA-congres: 'Het voetbal heeft gewonnen'
items.summary: Tijdens het UEFA-congres is in Brussel opgetogen gereageerd op het einde van de Super League. Woensdag werd bekend dat Real Madrid tot een akkoord was gekomen met de UEFA en de European Football Clubs (EFC), waarmee de beoogde nieuwe competitie definitief tot het verleden behoort en ook alle juridische geschillen zijn beëindigd.

items.title: Maswanhise warns 'watch out for us' in title race as Motherwell future addressed
items.summary: Tawanda Maswanhise has warned Hearts, Celtic and Rangers that Motherwell are still in the title race.

items.title: Nottingham Forest close in on Pereira appointment
items.summary: Nottingham Forest close to appointing former Wolves boss Vitor Pereira as their new head coach.

items.title: Antony openhartig: 'Ik speel al maanden met veel pijn'
items.summary: Antony heeft onthuld dat hij al maanden met pijn speelt bij Real Betis. De Braziliaanse aanvaller kampt met pubalgie, een hardnekkige blessure rond de schaamstreek en lies, die vooral bij explosieve acties zoals sprinten en schieten veel hinder kan veroorzaken. Volgens Antony speelt het probleem al sinds de wedstrijd tegen Getafe van 21 december 2025 en verklaart het ook waarom hij daarna een periode minder in vorm was.

items.title: Rondje Buitenland Extra: De Zerbi of Heitinga de redder van Tottenham?
items.summary: Nieuwe aflevering van Rondje Buitenland Extra! Genoeg te bespreken na een midweekse speelronde in de Premier League én het ontslag van verschillende trainers. Thomas Frank en Sean Dyche verloren hun baan bij respectievelijk Tottenham Hotspur en Nottingham Forest, maar ook Roberto De Zerbi is geen trainer meer van Olympique Marseille. Kenners buitenlands voetbal Süleyman Öztürk en Bas van den Hoven bespreken het uitgebreid.

items.title: Van Dijk prijst Konaté: 'Brobbey was negentig procent van de tijd bij hem'
items.summary: Ibrahima Konaté vocht woensdag verbeten duels uit met Brian Brobbey. De spits van Sunderland had een moeilijke avond tegen de collega van Virgil van Dijk, die hoopt dat de Fransman bij Liverpool blijft. ' Ibou is een van onze leiders.'

items.title: QPR and Crawley sued for £11.1m by former player who claims they did not protect him
items.summary: Amrit Bansal-McNulty claim relates to alleged racism Clubs deny wrongdoing in case being heard at tribunal Queens Park Rangers and Crawley Town are being sued for more than £11m by a former player who is claiming they failed to protect him from alleged racist banter that he says destroyed his career. The Championship club are joint defendants with Crawley in a claim currently being heard at the Central London Employment Tribunal, brought by the former Northern Ireland Under-21 international Amrit Bansal-McNulty, who is suing for £11.1m for a loss of opportunity and personal injury. Both clubs have denied wrongdoing. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Not a big club' - Ange reveals what is wrong at 'curious' Spurs...
items.summary: Tottenham’s week from hell got a whole lot worse on Thursday.

items.title: Did Dyche deserve the sack at Nottingham Forest?
items.summary: After Sean Dyche's reign lasts just 114 days, Nottingham Forest are on the hunt for a fourth head coach of the season - but as he hard done by?

items.title: Bring on the old guard to beat the drop: can Ange’s recall be right twist for Spurs? | Max Rushden
items.summary: If Tottenham are waiting for Pochettino part two, then season three of Postecoglou might bring the right survival vibes It’s panic time at the bottom of the Premier League, and if the past couple of days are anything to go by, probably don’t go following Ange Postecoglou into a job any time soon. Others who’ve followed it more closely can do Nottingham Forest and their 4 (four) managers. This is a piece about Tottenham Hotspur, or as I like to call them, my big team who win things. November 2023 feels like a lifetime ago. Spurs were top of the league. Angeball was at its peak. Dynamic free-flowing football – they were 1-0 up against Chelsea thanks to Dejan Kulusevski (injured). It’s the 14th minute, Spurs neatly play themselves out from back down the right, it breaks to Pape Sarr who rolls the ball to Destiny Udogie (injured), and Brennan Johnson (Crystal Palace) steams down the left. He plays a perfect first-time ball with his left foot into the path of Son Heung-min (LAFC), who rolls it home. Tottenham are 2-0 up against a team they lose to at least twice a season. Continue reading...

items.title: Tottenham are 'not a big club' - Postecoglou
items.summary: Former head coach Ange Postecoglou criticises Tottenham's transfer strategy in the wake of Thomas Frank's sacking.

items.title: Maloney expects more late drama in title race | Martinez to Celtic?
items.summary: It is the Scottish Premiership title race that refuses to follow the same old script - and Celtic assistant Shaun Maloney expects there to be plenty more twists in the tale before May.

items.title: FA to look at whether Jim Ratcliffe broke football rules by saying UK ‘colonised’ by immigrants
items.summary: FA assessing whether he brought game into disrepute Manchester United co-owner told by Starmer to apologise The Football Association will look at whether Jim Ratcliffe brought the game into disrepute through his claims that the UK has been “colonised” by immigrants. The Manchester United co-owner, a billionaire based in Monaco, caused widespread anger with his comments and has been called on by Keir Starmer to apologise. His remarks have not gone unnoticed within the FA, which will examine further before deciding whether he has broken any rules. Continue reading...

items.title: Hellberg on transforming Boro, belief in his side and his passion for English football
items.summary: The impact Kim Hellberg has had has been quite remarkable at Middlesbrough.

items.title: Crisis, concern or context needed? Coventry in a slump before 'make or break' Boro clash
items.summary: Cycle back to the final weekend of November and things looked very different for Coventry City.
